meta_title: For Founders: client workflow to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Client Workflow to Solve Project Stagnation
content.seo_description: Stop starting and start shipping. Learn a professional client workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to overcome project stagnation and drive revenue.
content.answer_block: To stop the cycle of unfinished projects, pivot from 'feature hunting' to a rigid client-feedback loop. By integrating Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you align every line of code with actual client needs, ensuring you build only what is profitable and deployable.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is littered with 'almost-finished' projects. You’ve felt the friction: the initial high of the V1, followed by the slow decay into feature-creep and technical debt. You aren't failing because you lack coding skills; you are failing because you lack a production-grade workflow that forces market validation.

Real founders don't build in a vacuum. They build in a loop. By shifting your perspective from 'coding' to 'client-driven engineering,' you turn your development process into a machine that produces viable, sellable assets. It’s time to stop tinkering and start shipping.

steps.title: Define the MVP Scope with Clients
steps.description: Stop guessing what the market wants. Use a structured discovery phase to narrow your scope to a single, high-value problem.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Help me distill my SaaS idea into a single core feature that generates immediate value. Provide a scope document that limits development to a 14-day sprint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review this scope document and identify any feature creep that could delay our 14-day launch.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Core Architecture
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a production-ready boilerplate that follows industry standards, saving you days of configuration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Next.js 15 boilerplate with Tailwind, Supabase integration, and Stripe checkout. Ensure the folder structure is modular for rapid feature additions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the project structure based on the Antigravity boilerplate output.'

steps.title: Build the Feedback Loop
steps.description: Implement a 'client-in-the-loop' mechanism where every minor release is pushed to a staging environment for instant feedback.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a simple feedback component for my app that logs user inputs directly to a Supabase table for review.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Implement the feedback component and ensure it captures user session metadata.'

steps.title: Iterative Refinement with Claude Code
steps.description: Don't write code manually when you can direct AI to do it. Use Claude Code to handle refactoring based on your client feedback logs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze these user feedback logs and suggest the three most critical UI/UX changes to improve conversion.
steps.claude_code_command: claude refactor 'Apply the three suggested UI changes to the current codebase while maintaining clean architecture.'

steps.title: Automated Deployment Pipeline
steps.description: Remove the friction of deployment. Automate your build process so that every commit is a potential product update.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Vercel-compatible CI/CD configuration that runs tests before every deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude setup 'Configure the deployment pipeline and run initial build checks.'

pro_tips: Never build a feature that hasn't been requested by at least two paying or prospective clients.
Use 'Time-Boxed Coding': If a feature takes more than 4 hours to implement, it is too complex for your current MVP.
Treat your codebase as a disposable asset; focus on the data model and the client interface, not the underlying framework.
Always keep a 'Backlog Cemetery' file for ideas you love but aren't shipping. It keeps your main branch clean.
Leverage Claude Code to write your unit tests; it forces you to define clear acceptance criteria before the code is even written.
content.next_steps: Deploy your staging environment to Vercel, invite 5 potential users to test the core feature, and iterate based on their feedback logs.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the strategy. Now you need the momentum to cross the finish line. Don't let another project sit in the dark. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
